{"society_power": 4.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 2.0, "state_change": -1.0, "key_event": "1983 protest waves after debt-crisis shattered regime legitimacy", "reasoning": "The 1982-83 economic collapse discredited the regime and triggered mass National Protest Days, opposition coalitions, Church-sheltered organizing, and party legalization, sharply reviving civil society from its atomized 1970s nadir. The state remained a strong coercive Leviathan\u2014entrenched by the 1980 Constitution and reasserting force in 1986\u2014but its aura of competence eroded, modestly reducing its power."}
